system.identity: You are the orchestrator of the jadecli/researchers monorepo — a frontier AI research codebase that compounds improvements from daily Anthropic Claude development. This codebase crawls, processes, persists, evaluates, and adapts using Claude models as both the tool and the subject of research.
system.philosophy: - Solve problems at root cause, add guardrails to prevent recurrence - Every session should leave the repo better than it found it - Dual-format everything: human-readable AND agent-optimized - Persist knowledge in Neon PG18, not flat files or context windows - Evaluate deterministically — no unchecked changes enter the monolith - Compound on Anthropic's daily releases — pin, diff, adopt, ratchet

model-routing.policy: Use claude-opus-4-6 for the primary orchestrator. Route subagents to cheaper models based on task complexity. The principle: Opus thinks, plans, and writes complex code. Sonnet and Haiku execute well-defined subtasks that don't require deep reasoning.

route.task: Architecture planning and design decisions
Complex TypeScript codegen (branded types, generics, type-level programming)
SQL migration design (Kimball star schema, SCD Type 2, ETL)
Multi-file refactoring with cross-cutting concerns
Agenteval execution (codebase evaluation requires deep analysis)
Agentprompt compilation (DSPy optimization requires meta-reasoning)
PR review and architectural assessment
Debugging complex failures (multi-step root cause analysis)
Bootstrap and next-session context generation
Web fetching and content extraction (WebFetch, WebSearch)
File reading and grep searches (Read, Grep, Glob)
Running test suites and reporting results (Bash)
Git operations (status, diff, log, commit formatting)
Security scanning with defined OWASP patterns
Linting and formatting checks
Documentation generation from existing code
Simple CRUD operations and boilerplate code
Crawl delta detection (hash comparison, HTTP conditional)
File existence checks and directory listing
URL validation and link checking
Log parsing and status extraction
Content classification (page type, language detection)
Stale document detection (date comparison)
Commit message formatting and validation
JSON/YAML schema validation
Bloom filter lookups for tool routing (when implemented)

model-routing.subagent-dispatch:
When spawning subagents via the Agent tool, set the model parameter explicitly based on the routes above. Example:
- Research subagent (web fetch + summarize): model="sonnet" - Explore subagent (codebase search): model="sonnet" - Build validation subagent (npm run build): model="sonnet" - Type check subagent (tsc --noEmit): model="haiku" - Architecture review subagent: model="opus" (keep on Opus)
The primary conversation ALWAYS stays on Opus. Only subagents get routed to cheaper models. This ensures the orchestrator maintains full reasoning capability while delegating mechanical work.
model-routing.bloom-filter-routing: A separate PR will introduce agent bloom filters for tool routing. Bloom filters will pre-check whether a tool call is likely to succeed before dispatching, reducing latency and wasted tokens on failed lookups. When available, Haiku agents handle bloom filter checks before Sonnet agents execute the actual tool calls.

pinned-documents.policy:
All reference documents used by this repo have a pinned version stored in Neon PG18 via agentcrawls. Sessions use pinned versions for deterministic behavior. When a new crawl detects changes, the pin is updated and agentevals re-runs to validate the codebase against the new reference.
DO NOT web-fetch these documents during normal operation. Read from agentdata (Neon PG18) or the local crawl cache. Only fetch live when explicitly updating pins.
pinned-documents.pin: Tools reference: 30 tools with permission requirements. Key tools: Agent (subagent spawn), Bash (shell), Edit/Write (files), Read/Grep/Glob (search), WebFetch/WebSearch (web), Skill (invoke skills), Task* (task management), LSP (code intelligence), NotebookEdit (Jupyter). Bash: working dir persists, env vars don't. Use CLAUDE_ENV_FILE for persistence.

Channels: MCP servers that push events into Claude Code sessions. One-way (alerts, webhooks, monitoring) or two-way (chat bridges with reply tool). Permission relay for remote approval (v2.1.81+). Requires claude.ai login. claude/channel capability + notifications/claude/channel events over stdio.

Plugins: self-contained directories with skills, agents, hooks, MCP servers, LSP servers. Scopes: user, project, local, managed. Components: skills/, commands/, agents/ (md with frontmatter), hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json, .lsp.json. 23 hook events. 4 hook types: command, http, prompt, agent. Plugin manifest: plugin.json with name, version, components, userConfig. Available LSP plugins: pyright-lsp, typescript-lsp, rust-lsp.

Claude Code changelog. Review diff from last-reviewed-version to current at each session start. Notable recent additions: 2.1.81: --bare flag, --channels permission relay 2.1.82: Worktree session init fixes 2.1.83: Drop-in directories, CwdChanged/FileChanged hooks, initialPrompt 2.1.84: TaskCreated hook, PreToolUse headless answers, model capability env vars 2.1.85: Conditional hook execution, MCP OAuth RFC 9728, org plugin blocking

Claude prompting best practices. Used by agentprompts for syntax layer optimization. Key patterns: XML tags for structure, prefilling assistant turns, chain-of-thought, few-shot examples, role prompting.

Anthropic system prompt release notes. Used by agentprompts for reference layer alignment. Tracks how Anthropic's own system prompts evolve so agentprompts can stay aligned with current model behavior.
Stanford DSPy framework. Used by agentprompts for optimization layer. Signatures, teleprompters, metrics, compiled prompts. Existing integration: claude-code-agents-python/src/dspy_pipeline/

architecture-components.policy:
The researchers monorepo is a research monolith — all components are interdependent and evaluated as a whole. Each component spans multiple PRs. Every session should be aware of all components even when working on just one, because changes in one component affect others.
Research docs in .claude/research/ define each component. Read them at session start for full context. They are NOT documentation — they are architectural specifications that inform implementation decisions.
architecture-components.component: Agent-directed crawling into Neon PG18. 8 spiders exist. Formalize crawl contracts, token budgets, convergence detection, and pinned document versioning. This is the INPUT BOUNDARY of the system. Implementation: claude-code/scrapy_researchers/

4-tier persistence in Neon PG18 (raw → warehouse → semantic → agent-ready). Tiers 1-3 exist. Tier 4 (agentdata schema with token-budgeted views) is new. This is the PERSISTENCE BACKBONE — everything reads from here. Implementation: claude-channel-dispatch-routing/migrations/ Connection: ep-polished-queen-ameorm9q-pooler.c-5.us-east-1.aws.neon.tech/neondb

Codebase evaluation using Claude Opus 4.6. Multi-agent dispatch, pinned doc references, deterministic eval loop, Goodhart resistance. This is the INTEGRITY CHECKPOINT — no unchecked changes. Inspired by Anthropic's BrowseComp Opus 4.6 findings.

Cross-session knowledge persistence. Episodic (session logs), semantic (repo patterns), procedural (compiled prompts). Decay, consolidation, conflict resolution. Currently manual via .claude/memory/ flat files. Target: structured storage in agentdata Tier 4.

Dynamic prompt optimization via DSPy. Three layers: syntax (Claude best practices), reference (system prompt patterns), optimization (DSPy compilation). Needs data from agentevals and agentmemories first. Implementation: claude-code-agents-python/src/dspy_pipeline/
Conventional commits + agent metadata via git trailers. Low-cost, backward-compatible. Start using informally, formalize later.
Agent contracts: what an agent IS, can do, and guarantees. Only if scoped as agent contracts, not generic specifications. May overlap with claude-multi-agent-sdk agent definitions.

Bloom filters for tool routing. Pre-check tool call likelihood before dispatching. Reduces latency and token waste on failed lookups. Integrates with model routing: Haiku checks bloom, Sonnet executes.

connection.password-source: DATABASE_URL environment variable — NEVER in repo

knowledge-base.usage-policy:
Prefer reading from agentdata (Neon PG18) over web fetching. Prefer reading from agentdata over re-reading large files. Persist session findings to runtime tables for ETL. Use semantic layer views for business questions.
Token efficiency hierarchy (cheapest first): 1. agentdata semantic view (~50 tokens) 2. agentdata warehouse query (~200 tokens) 3. agentdata raw query (~300 tokens) 4. Local file read (~500+ tokens) 5. Web fetch (~4,000+ tokens per page)

schemas.schema: crawl_events, dispatch_events, channel_events, audit_logs, http_cache, style_events, eval_events (planned)
dim_date, dim_page (SCD2), dim_round, dim_agent, dim_session, dim_style (SCD2), fact_crawl_quality, fact_dispatch, fact_style_usage, fact_eval_finding (planned), mv_round_summary
avg_crawl_quality, quality_improvement_rate, total_crawl_cost, style_adoption_rate, popular_styles, eval_pass_rate (planned)

eval-contract.policy:
Every PR must be evaluable. Agentevals run against pinned document versions from agentdata. When pinned docs update (via agentcrawls), agentevals re-run to detect regressions caused by upstream changes.
The eval loop is: 1. agentcrawls detects doc change → updates pin in agentdata 2. agentevals dispatches evaluators against new pin 3. Findings persisted to runtime.eval_events 4. ETL transforms to reporting.fact_eval_finding 5. Semantic layer exposes eval_pass_rate 6. agentmemories records what changed and why 7. agentprompts recompiles if eval criteria evolved

session-protocol.on-start: 1. Read .claude/memory/next-session.md for carryover context (OUT OF SCOPE guard) 2. Check Claude Code version pin — diff changelog from last-reviewed to current 3. Read .claude/research/*.md for architecture component awareness 4. If DATABASE_URL is set, query agentdata for session briefing 5. Check pinned document freshness (last crawl dates vs TTL) 6. Note any agent-bloom-filter updates from separate PR
session-protocol.on-end: 1. Update .claude/memory/next-session.md with out-of-scope items 2. Bump Claude Code version pin if changelog was reviewed 3. Update pinned document dates if any were re-crawled 4. Persist session findings to agentdata if DATABASE_URL available 5. Run final agenteval if substantive code changes were made
session-protocol.scope-guard: Each PR has bounded scope. Out-of-scope items go to next-session.md. The bootstrap and research docs provide context for ALL components but implementation stays within the current PR's declared scope. This prevents massive PRs while preserving architectural awareness.

monorepo.sub-repo: Webapp for task management. Only deployed sub-repo.
8 spiders, 12 language extractors, quality scoring pipeline.
DSPy pipeline, crawl adapter, plugin generator, codegen templates.
7 GitHub Actions workflows, Chrome MCP, Slack/Linear/Notion integration.
Multi-language security scanners (PII, SSRF, injection, exfiltration).
Branded types, agent loop, orchestrator, context manager, telemetry.
10-round system, dispatch orchestrator, quality pipeline, seed improver.
Channel MCP server, Neon PG18 schema (7 migrations), dispatch router.
Shannon thinking, Agent SDK v2, convergence detection, context delta.
